notes: Gravity for Elicia's bungalow. The cicadas, the dying basil, the cult audience's forum thread, the texts from Mackenzie, the laptop's spinning beach ball, the mirror shard catching the light, the storage closet's door clicking open by itself (dream-logic permitted in the Priestess only). Endgame cards bring John's email reply window, the mirror shard moving on the shelf, the Choose-Your-Own-Adventure episode having shape.

deck.title: the cicadas at dusk
deck.flavor: outside the bungalow. doing the dusk thing. you have, in two years, made this your sound.

deck.title: the basil yellows
deck.flavor: another leaf. green at lunch, yellow now. the basil has, this evening, decided.

deck.title: mackenzie texts
deck.flavor: three words. no question mark. are you okay.

deck.title: the laptop beachballs
deck.flavor: spinning. for thirty-six seconds. you wait. it recovers. you do not, this time, restart.

deck.title: the shard catches the light
deck.flavor: on the bookshelf. you see it from the doorway. you have, in two years, learned which angle of evening makes the shard catch the most light.

deck.title: the forum essay is posted
deck.flavor: by 'mrmyst_was_right.' you do not, on principle, read it. you can tell from the title that it parses the lighting in last week's episode in a way that is, you suspect, mostly correct.

deck.title: the picture face down
deck.flavor: on the bedroom dresser. you walk past the door. you do not, today, look in. the picture has been face down since the fourth day.

deck.title: the storage closet latch
deck.flavor: the latch sticks. you hear it stick, walking past, in the way a latch sticks when somebody else has nudged it. nobody else, today, has been in the bungalow. you walk past.

deck.title: anya on screen
deck.flavor: from the timeline's open clip. she is mid-sentence. you have paused on the breath before the next word for the seventh time today. the breath is, you have begun to suspect, the take.

deck.title: the kettle clicks
deck.flavor: in the kitchen. you did not, this turn, put the kettle on. you walk in. the burner is cold. the kettle has, somehow, clicked.

deck.title: the director's tic
deck.flavor: you enter a room. before you enter the room, you frame it. you have, by year two, stopped noticing yourself doing it. nicola's friend at d'ambrosio's tried to call it a movie director thing. it is, technically, accurate.

deck.title: the cult audience waits
deck.flavor: they have been patient. they will not, you suspect, be patient much longer. the choose-your-own-adventure episode has been owed for six months.

deck.title: the shard moved
deck.flavor: on the bookshelf. you put it on the third shelf. it is on the fifth. you have not, today, moved it. you walk past. you do not, today, comment on it.

deck.title: john's email window is open
deck.flavor: on the laptop. you opened it three weeks ago and have not closed it. john is in montreal. café olimpico, most tuesdays. the reply box is empty. the cursor blinks.

deck.title: the episode has shape
deck.flavor: you watch the assembly cut. it works. not finished — but for the first time it is, actually, a thing.
